title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Your heart pumps blood to your brain through blood vessels in your neck.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: These blood vessels, called arteries, branch into even smaller arteries that bring blood

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: to your brain cells.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Some of these small arteries join each other to form a loop known as the circle of willis.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The walls of the arteries contain special muscle and elastic layers that help them adapt

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: to the blood pulsing through them.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Sometimes diseases, injuries, or birth defects can cause a weak spot in the wall of the arteries

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: in the circle of willis.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: These weak spots usually form where arteries branch.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Over time, the pressure of blood flowing through the artery stresses the weak spot further.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The constant blood pressure can cause the weak spot to balloon out and form a bulge called

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: a cerebral aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: This cerebral aneurysm is the most common type called a sacular or berry aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Over time, the aneurysm may grow and push on nearby brain tissue or nerves.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Blood may also leak from the aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: If its wall gets too thin, the aneurysm can burst or rupture and spill high pressure

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: blood into the space around your brain.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The blood presses on your brain tissue and can cause injury to your brain cells.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Chemicals in the escaped blood can irritate other brain arteries and cause them to narrow.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: As a result, your brain can swell, leading to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Common symptoms of a ruptured cerebral aneurysm include sudden severe headache, sometimes

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: called a thunder clap headache, problems with your vision such as blurred or double vision,

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: nausea and vomiting, a stiff or painful neck, or confusion.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: An unruptured cerebral aneurysm may not cause symptoms, but large unruptured aneurysms

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: may cause headache and pain near or behind one eye, double or blurred vision, a drooping

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: eyelid and numbness or weakness on one side of your face.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: A ruptured cerebral aneurysm is a medical emergency if you are having symptoms seek immediate

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: medical attention.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: If you have a ruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend surgical clipping

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: or endovascular coiling.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In surgical clipping, your surgeon will make an opening in your skull, near your aneurysm,

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: and place a small metal clip onto the stem that connects the aneurysm to your artery.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The clip blocks blood flow into your aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In endovascular coiling, your doctor will put a plastic tube called a catheter into a large

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: artery in your groin.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Using a live X-ray view, your doctor will advance the catheter through your arteries until

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: it reaches the aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Through the catheter, your doctor will insert one or more flexible wires into your aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The wires will coil up and cause a blood clot to form inside your aneurysm, which will

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: block off the aneurysm from the rest of your artery.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In a similar manner, if you have an unruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: surgical clipping or endovascular coiling.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In addition, if your aneurysm is small and not causing pain or other symptoms, your doctor

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: may observe your aneurysm for a period of time before performing either of these procedures.
